content: Self-Refine's iterative unguided refinement framework can serve as a generalized backbone to integrate multiple inconsistent reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hierarchical self-improvement system that retains Self-Refine's advantage of no extra training or supervision while solving the common limitation of single-perspective bias in standalone self-reflection. This hierarchical structure lets lower-level iterations generate diverse candidate outputs from conflicting reflection perspectives, then upper-level Self-Refine cycles select and refine the most robust outcomes without labeled data.
